title: Nvidia device issue with podman >= 5.0
body:
### Issue Description

Hi guys,

I just want to report a nvidia issue with podman versions >= 5, or perhaps I miss a config or option somewhere.

I want to share gpus for my podman containers running podman services (podman in podman).
The gpus are recognized behind podman 4.9.4, but they're not for all versions of podman >=5.


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

Here is the context:
- I have several almalinux machines with gpus:  almalinux 9.6 + nvidia drivers installed / nvidia-smi (580.65.06 cuda 13.0 or 570.148.08 cude 12.8) nvidia-container-toolkit + nvidia-cdi-hook=1.17.6 (or 1.17.8...) + podman 4.9.4 (or 5.4...)
- creation of podman containers almalinux 9.6 and multiple options: --security-opt label=disable --security-opt unmask=/proc/* --device /dev/fuse --device nvidia.com/gpu=all --cap-add NET_ADMIN,NET_RAW,SYS_ADMIN,AUDIT_WRITE,mknod
- inside my containers, nvidia-smi is working fine and I generate the cdi config to discover the gpus: nvidia-ctk cdi generate --output /etc/cdi/nvidia.yaml
- then I run different podman services and below I will explain here one concrete example with Ollama.


**Behaviour working as expected with podman version 4.9.4-rhel:**
```
user@container:/# podman run --rm --device nvidia.com/gpu=all --security-opt=label=disable docker.io/nvidia/cuda:11.0.3-base-ubuntu20.04 nvidia-smi -L
GPU 0: NVIDIA RTX ... (UUID: GPU-1-...)
GPU 1: NVIDIA RTX ... (UUID: GPU-2-...)
```
My gpus are listed ok.

Then I launch Ollama:
```
user@container:/# podman run --rm --device nvidia.com/gpu=all ollama/ollama
level=INFO source=gpu.go:217 msg="looking for compatible GPUs"
level=INFO source=types.go:130 msg="inference compute" id=GPU-052baabb-005f-b1ea-3884-a7090966b563 library=cuda variant=v12 compute=8.6 driver=12.8 name="NVIDIA RTX ..." total="47.4 GiB" available="3.7 GiB"
level=INFO source=types.go:130 msg="inference compute" id=GPU-6e55251d-8081-6f80-d64a-165ce437807b library=cuda variant=v12 compute=8.6 driver=12.8 name="NVIDIA RTX ..." total="47.4 GiB" available="46.9 GiB"
```
My gpus are detected by the service Ollama so it will work as expected using the gpus.
**But, when I update my podman to the version 5.2.2 (or >= 5.0) with the same commands than in 4.9.4 the behaviour is different:**
```
user@container:/# podman run --rm --device nvidia.com/gpu=all --security-opt=label=disable docker.io/nvidia/cuda:11.0.3-base-ubuntu20.04 nvidia-smi -L
GPU 0: NVIDIA ... (UUID: GPU-1...)
GPU 1: NVIDIA ... (UUID: GPU-2...)
```
Means it can list my gpus (but listing doesn't mean it can use it...)

Then:
```
user@container:/# podman run --rm --gpus all ollama/ollama
level=INFO source=routes.go:1238 msg="server config" env="map[CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES: GPU_DEVICE_ORDINAL: HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES: HSA_OVERRIDE_GFX_VERSION: HTTPS_PROXY: HTTP_PROXY: NO_PROXY: OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH:4096 OLLAMA_DEBUG:INFO OLLAMA_FLASH_ATTENTION:false OLLAMA_GPU_OVERHEAD:0 OLLAMA_HOST:http://0.0.0.0:11434 OLLAMA_INTEL_GPU:false OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE:5m0s OLLAMA_KV_CACHE_TYPE: OLLAMA_LLM_LIBRARY: OLLAMA_LOAD_TIMEOUT:5m0s OLLAMA_MAX_LOADED_MODELS:0 OLLAMA_MAX_QUEUE:512 OLLAMA_MODELS:/root/.ollama/models OLLAMA_MULTIUSER_CACHE:false OLLAMA_NEW_ENGINE:false OLLAMA_NOHISTORY:false OLLAMA_NOPRUNE:false OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL:1 OLLAMA_ORIGINS:[http://localhost https://localhost http://localhost:* https://localhost:* http://127.0.0.1 https://127.0.0.1 http://127.0.0.1:* https://127.0.0.1:* http://0.0.0.0 https://0.0.0.0 http://0.0.0.0:* https://0.0.0.0:* app://* file://* tauri://* vscode-webview://* vscode-file://*] OLLAMA_SCHED_SPREAD:false ROCR_VISIBLE_DEVICES: http_proxy: https_proxy: no_proxy:]"
level=INFO source=images.go:476 msg="total blobs: 0"
level=INFO source=images.go:483 msg="total unused blobs removed: 0"
level=INFO source=routes.go:1291 msg="Listening on [::]:11434 (version 0.10.1)"
level=INFO source=gpu.go:217 msg="looking for compatible GPUs"
level=INFO source=gpu.go:612 msg="Unable to load cudart library /usr/lib64/libcuda.so.580.65.06: cuda driver library init failure: 304"
level=INFO source=gpu.go:377 msg="no compatible GPUs were discovered"
level=INFO source=types.go:130 msg="inference compute" id=0 library=cpu variant="" compute="" driver=0.0 name="" total="...GiB" available="...GiB"
```

I also added the old way to add the devices nvidia/gpu + capabilities + security options + env variables without any success:
```
user@container:/# podman run --rm --gpus all --device nvidia.com/gpu=all --cap-add NET_ADMIN,NET_RAW,SYS_ADMIN,AUDIT_WRITE,mknod --security-opt=label=disable --env-host -e NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=all -e NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES=all -e CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 ollama/ollama
time=2025-08-06T10:46:12.871Z level=INFO source=routes.go:1238 msg="server config" env="map[CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES:1 GPU_DEVICE_ORDINAL: HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES: HSA_OVERRIDE_GFX_VERSION: HTTPS_PROXY: HTTP_PROXY: NO_PROXY: OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH:4096 OLLAMA_DEBUG:INFO OLLAMA_FLASH_ATTENTION:false OLLAMA_GPU_OVERHEAD:0 OLLAMA_HOST:http://0.0.0.0:11434 OLLAMA_INTEL_GPU:false OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE:5m0s OLLAMA_KV_CACHE_TYPE: OLLAMA_LLM_LIBRARY: OLLAMA_LOAD_TIMEOUT:5m0s OLLAMA_MAX_LOADED_MODELS:0 OLLAMA_MAX_QUEUE:512 OLLAMA_MODELS:/root/.ollama/models OLLAMA_MULTIUSER_CACHE:false OLLAMA_NEW_ENGINE:false OLLAMA_NOHISTORY:false OLLAMA_NOPRUNE:false OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL:1 OLLAMA_ORIGINS:[http://localhost https://localhost http://localhost:* https://localhost:* http://127.0.0.1 https://127.0.0.1 http://127.0.0.1:* https://127.0.0.1:* http://0.0.0.0 https://0.0.0.0 http://0.0.0.0:* https://0.0.0.0:* app://* file://* tauri://* vscode-webview://* vscode-file://*] OLLAMA_SCHED_SPREAD:false ROCR_VISIBLE_DEVICES: http_proxy: https_proxy: no_proxy:]"
level=INFO source=images.go:476 msg="total blobs: 0"
level=INFO source=images.go:483 msg="total unused blobs removed: 0"
level=INFO source=routes.go:1291 msg="Listening on [::]:11434 (version 0.10.1)"
level=INFO source=gpu.go:217 msg="looking for compatible GPUs"
level=INFO source=gpu.go:612 msg="Unable to load cudart library /usr/lib64/libcuda.so.580.65.06: cuda driver library init failure: 304"
level=INFO source=gpu.go:377 msg="no compatible GPUs were discovered"
level=INFO source=types.go:130 msg="inference compute" id=0 library=cpu variant="" compute="" driver=0.0 name="" total="...GiB" available="...GiB"
```



### Describe the results you received

Cf above the outputs of my tests.

### Describe the results you expected

I explained what I was expected in the steps to reproduce the issue.

### podman info output


### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

Verifications I have also made:
 - the file /usr/lib64/libcuda.so.580.65.06 exists inside this container
 - the files /dev/nvidia0 and /dev/nvidia1 also exist
 - on my host: sudo modprobe nvidia_uvm && sudo modprobe nvidia && sudo modprobe nvidia_modeset && sudo modprobe nvidia_drm && sudo modprobe nvidia_uvm
 - using old versions of ollama suche as the 5.4 version
 - on my host: sudo setsebool container_use_devices=1  --> I only restarted my container, do I need to restart the podman service?
 - following https://github.com/ollama/ollama/blob/main/docs/gpu.md, my gpu are in the compatibility list (NVIDIA Professional)

What has changed between podman 4 and podman 5 regarding the usage of the nvidia devices?
Perhaps I have missed something?

I spent a lot of hours and days to find a solution, I would appreciate any suggestion :-)
Thanks for your help.

Regards

comments.body: I don't remember any obvious podman chnage that would explain this in any way. If you are sure a podman change causes that and not nvidia driver, cdi version, etc... the best way is to compile podman from source and then start a git bisect between the last working and first bad version and then see where you end up.

Im not sure this will help, but ill post it anyways.
I had a funky issue where Linuxserver.io Plex container in podman rootless could find the nvidia devices, but could not find the libraries, like libcuda.so.1, so HW transcoding always failed.
The solution that worked for me was to pass my own LD_LIBRARY_PATH to help the dynamic linker find the libraries.

For the plex container im using this fixed the issue for me `LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/usr/local/nvidia/lib64:/usr/local/nvidia/lib:/usr/lib/plexmediaserver/lib:/usr/lib:/lib`

From what i can read from your posts, it seems the issue is the same, the container can find the devices, but the app seems to be unable to load the libraries.
`Unable to load cudart library /usr/lib64/libcuda.so.580.65.06`

Good luck friend.

Hi I had a similar problem device was listed inside the container nvidia-smi was working correctly but as soon as I tried to use the encode it would error out.
What worked for me on podman 5.4.2 was:
```
sudo nvidia-ctk cdi generate --device-name-strategy=type-index --output=/etc/cdi/nvidia.yaml
nvidia-ctk cdi list
  INFO[0000] Found 2 CDI devices
  nvidia.com/gpu=all
  nvidia.com/gpu=gpu0
```
And then because I use podman play kube I changed the definition to:
```
...
        resources:
          requests:
            nvidia.com/gpu=gpu0: 1
```
Hope it helps somebody.
